channel.title: Home on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Memo

item.title: Android x86
item.description: Android x86 # System Setup # Host: Linux KVM Guest: Android x86, Lineage OS Install # Download OS image (*.iso) # Download *.iso from https://www.android-x86.org/ Configuration of Virtual Machine Manager # Install #

item.title: File Compression (tar, zip, lzh, bz2)
item.description: File Compression (tar, zip, lzh, bz2) # Usage # Extension Compression Decompression zip zip -r output.zip dir unzip input.zip tar tar -cvf output.tar dir tar -xvf input.tar tar.gz tar -acvf output.tar.gz dir tar -xvf input.tar.gz tar.bz2 tar -acvf output.tar.bz2 dir tar -xvf input.tar.bz2 tar.xz tar -acvf output.tar.xz dir tar -xvf input.tar.xz lzh lha -c output.

item.title: GitHub CLI
item.description: GitHub CLI # In this page, I will explain how to install, configure and use the GitHub CLI. Install # Use the following command to install GitHub CLI. Arch sudo pacman -S github-cli Ubuntu For Ubuntu, it is not possible to install directly from apt, so you will have to install via snap. sudo apt install snapd sudo snap install gh Configuration # You can authenticate your account by executing the following command.

item.title: Initial Settings for Ubuntu
item.description: Initial Settings for Ubuntu # This article is a guide to help you quickly configure the initial settings when you install the desktop version of Ubuntu in VirtualBox. It is assumed that English is selected as the language setting during installation, and Minimal Install is selected. wget http://forest0923.github.io/memo/docs/ubuntu/initial-settings/ubuntu-initial-settings.sh chmod +x ubuntu-initial-settings.sh ./ubuntu-initial-settings.sh Systems Setup # Ubuntu 18.04 LTS (minimal install) Ubuntu 20.04 LTS (minimal install) Initial Settings # Set the Time to Sleep # Set the time as follows.

item.title: Install Arch Linux (BTRFS + Timeshift)
item.description: Install Arch Linux (BTRFS + Timeshift) # Installation manual for Arch Linux. This article describes how to install Arch Linux on BTRFS filesystem and save snapshots with Timeshift. System # Intel CPU UEFI Boot Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: Install Curry
item.description: Install Curry # Curry is a language that has features of both functional and logic programming languages. PAKCS is an interpreter that can execute programs written in Curry. PAKCS converts Curry code into Prolog and executes it. Install # Arch paru -S pakcs Ubuntu sudo apt-add-repository ppa:swi-prolog/stable sudo apt update sudo apt install swi-prolog sudo apt install pakcs download site Basic Usage # Sample program: -- family.

item.title: Install Haskell
item.description: Install Haskell # This is a guide to installing Haskell using Stack. Install Stack # Stack is a tool for managing haskell projects, installing compilers and packages, and building projects. The installation is done as follows. Arch sudo pacman -S stack Ubuntu curl -sSL https://get.haskellstack.org/ | sh stack upgrade Basic Usage # To create a new project, run stack new. stack new hs-proj When you create a new project, the following directory structure will be created, and you can write the main code in Main.

item.title: Install Kernel
item.description: Install Kernel # This is a step-by-step guide on how to install kernel for those who are new to kernel development. System Setup # OS: Ubuntu 18.04 (64bit) Current kernel: 5.3.0-42-generic Installed kernel: 5.4.1 Step-by-step Instructions # 1. Preparing Source Code # In this article, I assume that /usr/src is a working directory. First of all, change directory to /usr/src and download kernel source code with the following command.

item.title: Install KVM
item.description: Install KVM # Install # Arch sudo pacman -S virt-manager qemu qemu-arch-extra ovmf vde2 \ ebtables dnsmasq bridge-utils openbsd-netcat Ubuntu sudo apt install qemu-kvm libvirt-daemon-system \ libvirt-clients bridge-utils virtinst Configrations # Configure the systemd service to automatically enable the libvirt daemon. sudo systemctl enable libvirtd.service sudo systemctl start libvirtd.service Create an xml file to configure the port and IP address settings. sudo vim br0.xml <network> <name>br0</name> <forward mode='nat'> <nat> <port start='1024' end='65535'/> </nat> </forward> <bridge name='br0' stp='on' delay='0'/> <ip address='192.

item.title: Install OCaml
item.description: Install OCaml # Install # Install opam, the package manager for OCaml. sudo pacman -S opam Dune, the OCaml build tool, is installed using opam. opam install dune Basic Usage # REPL can be used by running ocaml. % ocaml OCaml version 4.13.1 # 1+1;; - : int = 2 # exit 0;; To create a new project using Dune, run dune init. dune init exe myproj .

item.title: Install Prolog
item.description: Install Prolog # Install # Commands: sudo pacman -S swi-prolog Basic Usage # Sample program: %% family.pl parent(alice). parent(bob). child(charlie). child(dave). parent_child(alice, charlie). parent_child(alice, dave). parent_child(bob, charlie). parent_child(bob, dave). sibling(X,Y) :- parent_child(Z, X), parent_child(Z, Y). Start interactive shell with swipl. $ swipl Welcome to SWI-Prolog (threaded, 64 bits, version 8.2.3) SWI-Prolog comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. This is free software.

item.title: Install Rust
item.description: Install Rust # Install # Use rustup to install and version control rust. After installing rustup, add $HOME/.cargo/bin to your PATH. Arch sudo pacman -S rustup Otherwise curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh rustup # To update rustc and other programs using rustup, run rustup update. rustup update If you want to update rustup itself, you can update it with the package manager of your distribution or run rustup self update.

item.title: Install TeX Live
item.description: Install TeX Live # This is a startup guide for using LaTeX. System Environment # OS: Linux Install # TeX Live # Install texlive and language packages with the following command. # Arch sudo pacman -S texlive-most texlive-langjapanese # ubuntu sudo apt install texlive-full jListing # If you want to add source code that contains Japanese, you need to download jlisting from https://osdn.net/projects/mytexpert/downloads/26068/jlisting.sty.bz2/ and extract it.

item.title: Public Key Cryptography
item.description: Public Key Cryptography # Public key cryptography is a cryptographic scheme that uses two keys, a public key and a private key. It is used for signature, key exchange and etc. The security is ensured by using the difficulty of the calculation. For example, given $x$, it is easy to compute $y$ such that $y=f(x)$, but it is difficult to compute $x$ from $y$. RSA # RSA is a cryptosystem that relies on the difficulty of prime factorization.

item.title: Shell Script Cheat Sheet
item.description: Shell Script Cheat Sheet # Array # Test code: #!/bin/sh array=(0 1 2 3) echo $array echo ${array[0]} echo ${array[@]} Output: 0 0 0 1 2 if # Test code: #!/bin/sh val0=0 val1=1 # if [ $val0 = 0 ]; then echo val0 = 0 elif [ $val0 = 1 ]; then echo val0 = 1 else echo otherwise fi # AND if [ $val0 = 0 ] && [ $val1 !

item.title: Syntax Highlighting
item.description: Syntax Highlighting # How to post the source code with syntax highlighting. Gist # Using GitHub Gist, you can put the source code by simply pasting Script tags. Copy the tag in the upper right corner of the Gist repository and paste it into the body to insert the source code. <script src="https://gist.github.com/Forest0923/dad279ab557ae30bc51e4a9d8cf5bd7d.js"></script> prism.js # prism.js is a syntax highlighting tool implemented in JavaScript. To use prism.

item.title: Install Kernel (Arch Linux ver.)
item.description: Install Kernel (Arch Linux ver.) # This is a note of my development of a Linux kernel on Arch Linux. Basically, I use Ubuntu for my research, but when I tried to install a custom kernel on Arch Linux, I found that the same procedure as on Ubuntu did not work. I will show you how to compile and install without using PKGBUILD. Download Source Code # The kernel source code was downloaded from the following URL.

item.title: Connect to GitHub with SSH
item.description: Connect to GitHub with SSH # SSH Key Generation # Executing ssh-keygen as follows will generate a key using the algorithm you have selected. EdDSA ssh-keygen -t ed25519 RSA ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 ECDSA ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 521 If you do not make any changes during the generation, the private key id_[algorithm] and the public key id_[algorithm].pub will be generated in ~/.ssh. Registering a Private Key to SSH Agent # By registering your key with SSH Agent, you will not be asked for your passphrase every time.

item.title: Expressions like LaTeX
item.description: Beautiful Mathmatical Expressions # MathJax # MathJax is a library for displaying beautiful mathematical expressions like LaTeX. To use it, you need to add the following code. <script src="https://polyfill.io/v3/polyfill.min.js?features=es6"></script> <script id="MathJax-script" async src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-mml-chtml.js"></script> Expressions can be written by enclosing them with \[\], \begin{align}\end{align} or etc. Examples: \[a^2 + b^2 = c^2\] or \begin{align} 1 &= e^{i\theta}e^{-i\theta}\\ &= \left(\cos{\theta}+i\sin{\theta}\right) \left(\cos{\theta}-i\sin{\theta}\right)\\ &= \sin^2{\theta}+\cos^2{\theta} \end{align} Inline expressions can be written by enclosing the expression with $ or \(\).

item.title: GPG Key
item.description: GNU Privacy Guard # GnuPG is the OpenPGP standard for encrypting and signing data and communications. How to Use GnuPG # List registered keys # List public keys： gpg --list-keys List private keys: gpg --list-secret-keys Add Keys # gpg --keyserver keys.gnupg.net --recv-keys [key id] Remove Keys # gpg --delete-keys [key id] Key Servers # keys.openpgp.org keys.gnupg.net keys.mailvelope.com pgp.mit.edu

item.title: How to Use virsh
item.description: How to Use virsh # virsh is a tool for managing KVM and Guest VMs via the CLI. This page describes how to use virsh to manage KVM and VMs. Change the Number of vCPU Cores # sudo virsh setvcpus [vm_name] [vcpu_num] --config --maximum sudo virsh setvcpus [vm_name] [vcpu_num] --config

item.title: Inline Assembly
item.description: Inline Assembly in Rust # llvm_asm!() # Sample code: #![feature(llvm_asm)] fn main() { let i: u64 = 3; let j: u64 = 5; let o: u64; unsafe { llvm_asm!("mov $1, $0; add $2, $0;" : "=r"(o) : "{bx}"(i), "{cx}"(j)); } println!("{}", o); } Output: % rustup run nightly cargo run Compiling asm_test v0.1.0 (/home/mori/playground/rust/asm_test) Finished dev [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 0.

item.title: Install Arch Linux (Multi-disks + BTRFS + Snapper)
item.description: Install Arch Linux (Multi-disks + BTRFS + Snapper) # Installation manual for Arch Linux. This article describes how to install Arch Linux on BTRFS filesystem and save snapshots with Snapper. System # Intel CPU UEFI Boot Disks /dev/vda /dev/vdb /dev/vdc Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: LaTeX Cheat Sheet
item.description: LaTeX Cheat Sheet # Expressions # Expression Number # If you need an expression number: \begin{eqnarray} y=\alpha x + \beta \end{eqnarray} $$ y=\alpha x + \beta \tag{1} $$ If you do not need an expression number: \[ y=\alpha x + \beta \] $$ y=\alpha x + \beta $$ Align Equations # Use & to align multiple equations at equal positions.

item.title: nkf
item.description: nkf # Install # Arch paru -S nkf Ubuntu sudo apt install nkf Usage # To convert any file to UTF-8 + LF: nkf -w -Lu --overwrite input_file To convert C files in current directory to UTF-8 + LF: find . -iname "*.c" -type f -print0 | xargs -0 nkf -w -LF --overwrite

item.title: Remove Kernel
item.description: Remove Kernel # If the installed kernel no longer in use, you may want to remove it. This is introduction to remove kernel. How to remove kernel # To remove the kernel, you have to remove the following entries. /boot/vmlinuz-[version] /boot/initrd.img-[version] /boot/System.map-[version] /boot/config-[version] /lib/modules/[version]/* /var/lib/initramfs_tools/[version] rm /boot/vmlinuz-[version] rm /boot/initrd.img-[version] rm /boot/System.map-[version] rm /boot/config-[version] rm -rf /lib/modules/[version]/* rm /var/lib/initramfs_tools/[version] Then don’t forget to update the grub configuration. grub-mkconfig -o /boot/grub/grub.

item.title: Reverse Engineering (APK)
item.description: Reverse Engineering (APK) # This is a page about tools for analyzing APK files, which are application packages for Android, and how to use them. Tools # Apktool # A tool for reverse engineering APKs. Arch paru -S android-apktool paru -S android-apktool-git dex2jar # A tool to convert Android’s dex format to Java’s class. Arch paru -S dex2jar paru -S dex2jar-git jd-cli # CLI tool for Java Decompiler.

item.title: Configurations for Gpu Passthrough
item.description: Configurations for Gpu Passthrough # GPU passthrough is a technology that allows VMs to directly access hardware (GPU), and is expected to provide high graphics performance even in a virtualized environment. This page describes how to pass-through an Nvidia GPU to Windows 10 on KVM. System # Host: Arch Linux Guest: Windows 10 CPU: Intel Core-i9 9900K (8 cores, 16 threads) GPT: Nvidia RTX 2060 Memory: 32 GB Grub # Change the Grub configuration and generate a config file with grub-mkconfig.

item.title: Dual Booting (mono disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux)
item.description: Dual Booting (mono disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux) # System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel Storage: /dev/sda Preparation in Windows 10 # First, install Windows 10, then run Disk Management to create a free space for the Linux installation. The layout before and after creating the free partition will look like this. Before: After: Then, install Arch Linux as follows.

item.title: Dual Booting (multi-disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux)
item.description: Dual Booting (multi-disk + Windows10 and Arch Linux) # System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel GPU: Nvidia Storage: /dev/sda: Windows sub /dev/sdb: Windows main /dev/sdc: Linux Install # Change Keymaps # Change the keymap to the one you use for the installation. For a Japanese keyboard, use jp106. loadkeys jp106 Time Settings # Execute the following command to use the NTP (Network Time Protocol).

item.title: ffmpeg
item.description: ffmpeg # Install # Arch sudo pacman -S ffmpeg Ubuntu sudo apt install ffmpeg MP4 Manipulation with ffmpeg # Command Description ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -crf 30 output.mp4 Compression. The higher the crf value, the higher the compression ratio. ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -an output.mp4 Mute audio.

item.title: Implementing Original Systemcalls
item.description: Implementing Original Systemcalls # This is a tutorial of implementing new systemcalls. System Setup # Linux kernel 5.8.13 CPU: x86_64 Step-by-step instructions # 1. Register New Systemcall # Add a new entry in the arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl. You can use empty systemcall number and register hello_syscall as follows. 437 common openat2 sys_openat2 438 common pidfd_getfd sys_pidfd_getfd 439 common faccessat2 sys_faccessat2 +440 common hello_syscall sys_hello_syscall # # x32-specific system call numbers start at 512 to avoid cache impact 2.

item.title: Introduction to Creating Your Own OS with Ubuntu
item.description: Introduction to Creating Your Own OS with Ubuntu # This article is tips for creating HariboteOS. My HariboteOS System Setup # OS: Ubuntu 18.04 Tools Used # Binary editor # hexedit sudo apt install hexedit ghex sudo apt install ghex Assembler # nasm sudo apt install nasm nasm input.asm -o output (-l output.lst) C Compiler # gcc gcc -march=i486 -m32 -fno-pie -nostdlib -T [linkerfile] -g *.

item.title: Mermaid
item.description: Mermaid # Mermaid.js is a tool for displaying diagrams or graphs in a text-based format similar to Markdown. This article explains how to install it and its syntax. Setup # It can be used by adding the following script to the page, referring to Mermaid.js. <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid/dist/mermaid.min.js"></script> <script> mermaid.initialize({ startOnLoad: true }); </script> Figures can be drawn by Mermaid.js by writing them in <div class="mermaid">. <div class="mermaid"> flowchart A-->B </div> Syntax # Flowchart # A flowchart can be written by using flowchart.

item.title: System Call
item.description: System Call # Use system-call crate # Add a dependency to Cargo.toml: [dependencies] system-call="0.1.3" Sample code: #[macro_use] extern crate syscall; fn main() { let mut pid; let cpid; let mut val = 0; unsafe { pid = syscall!(GETPID); } println!("before: val is {}, pid is {}", val, pid); unsafe { cpid = syscall!(FORK); } if cpid < 0 { println!

item.title: Usecases of Git
item.description: Usecases of Git # Cancel git add # git reset # or git reset <file> Cancel git commit # # Reset contents git reset <tag> --hard # Reset commit and keep changes git reset <tag> --soft View previous commit # git checkout <tag>

item.title: Using Custom Style File
item.description:
Using Custom Style File # Create style file (~.sty) # Create new command # 
ewcomand and enewcommand allow you to define new command. 
ewcommand{\command-name}[argument-number][default]{definition} enewcommand{\command-name}[argument-number][default]{definition} Example: 
ewcommand{\myfig}[4][width=5cm]{  egin{figure}[H] \centering \includegraphics[#1]{#2} \caption{#3} \label{fig:#4} \end{figure} } Create new environment # 
ewenvironment and enewenvironment allow you to define new environment. 
ewenvironment{environment-name}[argument-number][default]{before}{after} enewenvironment{environment-name}[argument-number][default]{before}{after} Example: \definecolor{code-bg}{rgb}{0.95, 0.95, 0.95} 
ewenvironment{code}[2][] {\VerbatimEnvironment egin{minted}[breaklines, frame=lines, bgcolor=code-bg, #1]{#2}} {\end{minted}} Add style files # Put your file under /usr/local/share/texmf/tex/latex and execute mktexlsr as a root user.

item.title: Configurations for Single Root I/O Virtualization
item.description: Configurations for Single Root I/O Virtualization # SR-IOV (Single Root I/O Virtualization) is a hardware feature that allows a single physical device to be recognized by a hypervisor as if it were multiple separate devices. For example, a machine with a single NIC that supports SR-IOV can be recognized as if it has multiple NICs by using SR-IOV, and each NIC can be passed through to the VM. This page contains some notes on using SR-IOV with KVM.

item.title: Implementing Original Hypercalls
item.description: Implementing Original Hypercalls # This is a tutorial of implementing new hypercalls. System Setup # CPU: x86_64 Hypervisor: Linux KVM 5.8.13 Guest: Linux 5.8.13 Step-by-step instructions # 1. Add new hypercall entry in KVM # Register new hypercall number at the include/uapi/linux/kvm_para.h. #define KVM_HC_CLOCK_PAIRING 9 #define KVM_HC_SEND_IPI 10 #define KVM_HC_SCHED_YIELD 11 +#define KVM_HC_HELLO_HYPERCALL0 12 +#define KVM_HC_HELLO_HYPERCALL1 13 /* * hypercalls use architecture specific Hypercall occurs VMCALL that is one of the reason of VM Exit.

item.title: platformio
item.description: platformio # Install # PlatformIO is written in Python. You can install following command: pip3 install platformio Basic usage # You can use pio instead of platformio. Command Description pio boards [Query] You can see supported boards. pio init --board [ID] Initialize current directory. pio lib search [Query] Search library. pio lib show [Query] Show description of the library.

item.title: Rust Cheat Sheet
item.description: Rust Cheat Sheet #

item.title: SSH
item.description: SSH # SSH stands for Secure Shell, a protocol that allows you to communicate with a computer securely from a remote location. How to use SSH (client) # Install # Arch sudo pacman -S openssh Ubuntu sudo apt install openssh-client Key Generation # Generate a key with ssh-keygen. RSA ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 ECDSA ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 521 EdDSA ssh-keygen -t ed25519 By default, the public key id_[algorithm].

item.title: Submodule
item.description: Submodule # The submodule is a feature that allows you to include another Git repository as a sub directory in your repository. If you want to use the contents of a library or another project you are developing, you can include it in the project while managing it separately from the main project. Adding Submodule # To add a submodule, use the following command. git submodule add The information of the repository added as a submodule is described in .

item.title: Using minted for Syntax Highlighting
item.description: Using minted for Syntax highlighting # About # minted is a package that enable to add syntax highlight by using Pygments library. Installation # Pygments is written in Python and can be installed by pip pip install Pygments Package minted is already installed if you use texlive. Usage # At the preamble add some commands as follows. \usepackage[cache=false]{minted} \usepackage{xcolor} \definecolor{code-bg}{rgb}{0.95, 0.95, 0.95} You can then use syntax highlighting in your document as follows.

item.title: Desktop Environment
item.description: Desktop Environment # This is a tutorial for installing desktop environment. Install # Graphics Driver # Install graphics driver. Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Nvidia sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Display Server # Install xorg as a display server. A display server is software that passes input and output using a GUI such as Gnome to the OS and hardware.

item.title: fwupd
item.description: fwupd # Install # Arch sudo pacman -S fwupd Ubuntu sudo apt install fwupd Usage # Refresh data from server: fwupdmgr refresh Check device for updates: fwupdmgr get-updates Update and install: fwupdmgr update Troubleshooting # If the update does not apply, follow the steps below. Download .cab file Install: fwupdmgr install [path-to-.cab]

item.title: LFS (Large File Storage)
item.description: LFS (Large File Storage) # The maximum file size that can be uploaded to github is 100MB. Git Large File Storage enables to upload large file that is larger than 100MB. Install # Install git-lfs with following commands. Arch sudo pacman -S git-lfs Ubuntu sudo apt install git-lfs Use the following command to confirm that the installation is complete. git lfs install How to Use # Assume that you have a file smaller than 100MB named hello.

item.title: find and grep
item.description: find and grep # Source Code Reading with find + grep # find . -type f \( -name '*.c' -o -name '*.h' \) -print0 | xargs -0 grep -C 3 -Hni 'root_hpa'

item.title: Installing BSPWM
item.description: Installing BSPWM # Install and customize a Tile-type window manager called BSPWM. Install and Configurations # Graphics Driver # Install graphics driver. Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Nvidia sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms BSPWM # Install display server, bspwm and etc. sudo pacman -S xorg xorg-xinit bspwm picom dmenu sxhkd nitrogen arandr Install a terminal emulator and a browser.

item.title: Useful Links for Studying Git
item.description: Useful Links for Studying Git # Backlog Git Tutorial Learn Git Branching

item.title: Document Generator (doxygen, sourcetrail)
item.description: Document Generator # Doxygen # Doxygen Sourcetrail # Sourcetrail

item.title: Install Applications
item.description: Install Applications # Package Manager # Paru git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/paru cd paru makepkg -si Yay git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git cd yay makepkg -si Shell # zsh sudo pacman -S zsh zsh-completions chsh -s $(which zsh) # or chsh -s /bin/zsh Terminal Emulator # Alacritty sudo pacman -S alacritty Xfce4 terminal sudo pacman -S xfce4-terminal Gnome terminal paru -S gnome-terminal-transparency Terminal multiplexer # tmux sudo pacman -S tmux Editor # VSCode paru -S visual-studio-code-bin vim sudo pacman -S gvim emacs sudo pacman -S emacs-nox Backup # Timeshift paru -S timeshift Browser # Brave browser paru -S brave-bin Virtual Machine Monitor # KVM

item.title: dd
item.description: dd # Bootable USB # dd bs=4M if=/path/to/archlinux.iso of=/dev/sdx status=progress && sync

item.title: Power Management
item.description: Power Management # This is a memo about power management. TLP # TLP is a power management tool for Linux. Once installed and activated, it can be used without any special configuration. Install # sudo pacman -S tlp Settings # If you are using BTRFS as a file system, you should make the following settings because the file system may be corrupted. sudo vim /etc/default/tlp SATA_LINKPWR_ON_BAT=max_performance Enable TLP # Enable TLP by running following commands.

item.title: Arch Build System
item.description: Arch Build System # ABS is a system that automates the building of packages from source code; it uses makepkg to build packages based on information in PKGBUILD, and pacman to install them. Install # Install asp to use ABS. sudo pacman -S asp Usage (kernel build) # The following is an example of how to build a kernel. Create build/ as a working directory. mkdir build cd build Download the PKGBUILD for the Linux kernel.

item.title: Configurations for Spotify
item.description: Configurations for Spotify # This is a page about Spotify settings and troubleshooting. HiDPI # If you use a high resolution monitor, the default UI is too small, so change the settings to make it easier to see. By modifying /usr/share/applications/spotify.desktop as follows, you can change the scale of Spotify when it is launched in the GUI. -Exec=spotify %U +Exec=spotify --force-device-scale-factor=1.5 %U Download Error # Since Spotify has a lot of download errors, I’ll write down some of the errors and solutions below.

item.title: Compress initramfs
item.description: Compress initramfs # In some cases, the /boot partition is too small for the initramfs to fit. This makes it impossible to install multiple kernels, so the solution is to change the compression method. Edit /etc/initramfs-tools/initramfs.conf and change the compression method to xz (xz has a higher compression ratio, but slows down the decompression speed). sudo vim /etc/initramfs-tools/initramfs.conf - MODULES=most + MODULES=dep - COMPRESS=lz4 + COMPRESS=xz Install xz-utils and update initramfs.

item.title: Gnome Configurations
GRUB Resolution Settings
item.description: GRUB Resolution Settings # System # Arch CUI Check Available Resolutions # Open a GRUB terminal to check available resolutions. You can get a list of available resolution by running videoinfo. Setting the Resolution # Modify /etc/default/grub to set the resolution, referring to the list. Basically, you should change GRUB_GFXMODE and GRUB_GFX_PAYLOAD_LINUX as follows. However, in my environment, if I did not add nomodeset to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT, the resolution remains low after kernel boot.

item.title: Hack
item.description: Hack # Links # Try Hack Me Hack the Box Security Contest ksnctf Dictionary Attack # https://wiki.skullsecurity.org/index.php/Passwords https://web.archive.org/web/20120207113205/http://www.insidepro.com/eng/download.shtml https://www.passwordrandom.com/most-popular-passwords https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists/tree/master/Passwords https://crackstation.net/crackstation-wordlist-password-cracking-dictionary.htm

item.title: Japanese Input
item.description: Japanese Input # This is a memo on how to set up Japanese input using Fcitx (Flexible Input Method Framework) and Mozc. Install # fcitx5 fcitx5 is a successor to fcitx. sudo pacman -S fcitx5-mozc fcitx5-im fcitx5-configtool fcitx sudo pacman -S fcitx-mozc fcitx-im fcitx-configtool Configurations # Add Mozc to the input method in fcitx configuration tool. Then, create ~/.pam_environment. vim ~/.pam_environment + GTK_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx + QT_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx + XMODIFIERS DEFAULT=\@im=fcitx + SDL_IM_MODULE DEFAULT=fcitx If this does not work, execute the following command.

item.title: Keymaps
item.description: Keymaps # This is a note on how to assign other keys to keys such as Caps lock. Create Custom Key Mapping # Create a directory to store the keymap. sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps Use dumpkeys to get the original keymaps and save it in my-keymaps.map. sudo dumpkeys | sudo tee /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map Modify /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map to change Caps lock to control. sudo vim /usr/local/share/kbd/keymaps/my-keymaps.map - keycode 58 = Caps_Lock + keycode 58 = Control Create /etc/vconsole.

item.title: L2TP IPsec
item.description: L2TP IPsec # paru -S xl2tpd strongswan networkmanager-l2tp

item.title: Web Tools
item.description: Web Tools #
